title: Modify Fields in Your Atlas Entity-Relationship Diagram

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
The following steps describe how you can add, edit, and remove fields
in your entity-relationship diagram:

sections.heading: Step 1: Add a new field.
sections.body:
To add a new field, click the plus Plus icon in the upper-right
corner of one of your collection nodes. A new field appears in the
collection node.

Once you add a field, you can edit the field's properties, including:

- Renaming the field.

- Changing the field's data type.

- Adding relationships.

sections.heading: Step 2:  Rename a field.
sections.body:
Under the **Name** section, specify a new name for your selected
field.

sections.heading: Step 3:  Change a field's data type.
sections.body:
In the **Datatype** drop-down menu, select the datatypes that you
want to assign to the field. Because data in MongoDB has a flexible schema
model, you can specify multiple data types for a field. If a field
contains multiple data types, Atlas labels the field as
**(mixed)** in the diagram.

sections.heading: Step 4:  Delete a field.
sections.body: In the side panel, click the trash Trash icon.

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
